name: Project Grudge
description: Project Grudge was a short-lived US Air Force (USAF) project established in late 1948 to investigate unidentified flying objects (UFOs). It succeeded Project Sign, inheriting its files and personnel. Proponents argue Project Grudge was a deliberate effort to debunk UFO sightings and conceal evidence of extraterrestrial visitation, motivated by a desire to prevent public panic and maintain national security. Evidence cited includes the project's negative conclusions regarding UFO reports, alleged suppression of dissenting opinions within the project team, and claims that some sightings were deliberately misidentified or ignored. Mainstream historians and scientists generally view Project Grudge as a legitimate, though ultimately flawed, attempt to investigate UFOs using the limited scientific tools and understanding available at the time. They point to the lack of concrete evidence supporting claims of a deliberate cover-up and emphasize the project's eventual conclusion that most UFO sightings could be explained as misidentified conventional objects or natural phenomena. Criticisms include the project's perceived lack of scientific rigor, potential biases influencing its investigations, and the relatively short period it was active before being superseded by Project Blue Book.
subtitle: Early USAF UFO investigation project

claims.claim: Project Grudge was a deliberate cover-up operation.
claims.evidence: Alleged suppression of dissenting opinions and misidentification of sightings.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence and alternative explanations for reported actions.

claims.claim: Project Grudge dismissed legitimate UFO sightings as misidentified objects or hoaxes.
claims.evidence: The project's negative conclusions regarding UFO reports.
claims.criticism: Many reported sightings lacked sufficient evidence for definitive conclusions and could be plausibly explained by conventional means.

claims.claim: Project Grudge aimed to prevent public panic regarding UFOs.
claims.evidence: Statements suggesting a need to maintain public order and national security.
claims.criticism: While concern about public reaction was likely a factor, it does not necessarily indicate a deliberate cover-up.

keyFigures.name: Edward J. Ruppelt
keyFigures.role: Chief of Project Blue Book, successor to Grudge; initially involved.

keyFigures.name: J. Allen Hynek
keyFigures.role: Astronomer and consultant to Project Sign, Grudge, and Blue Book; later became a prominent UFO researcher and proponent of the extraterrestrial hypothesis.

mythologyConnections: Connections to folklore about aerial phenomena

timeline.event: Project Grudge is deemed ineffective and reorganized.
Project Blue Book replaces Project Grudge.

sources.author: Edward J. Ruppelt
sources.work: The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects
sources.title: Edward J. Ruppelt
